primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intercompany dividend receivables for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Explicitly restricts hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements to transactions with external parties only, prohibiting hedge accounting for intragroup items
Defines eligible hedged items as recognized assets/liabilities, firm commitments, forecast transactions, or net investments in foreign operations

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies that intragroup monetary items cannot be eliminated on consolidation and may create foreign currency exposure qualifying as hedged item in consolidated statements only when not fully eliminated
Provides guidance on hedged risk for net investment hedges and confirms hedge accounting may only apply to FX differences between functional currencies of parent and foreign operation

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses classification of financial instruments but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items
Contains disclosure requirements for hedge accounting but does not establish eligibility criteria for hedging relationships
Addresses measurement of non-cash dividend distributions but does not address hedge accounting treatment for dividend FX risk

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting treatment for intragroup foreign currency exposures
Fair value through profit or loss designation

approaches.label: Hedge accounting prohibited for intercompany dividends in consolidated statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 explicitly prohibits hedge accounting for intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements, requiring transactions to be with external parties

approaches.label: Hedge accounting permitted only in separate or individual financial statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 permits hedge accounting for intragroup items only in individual or separate financial statements of the entities involved

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss designation for dividend receivables
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9, entities may designate financial assets at fair value through profit or loss, which provides an alternative accounting treatment for the FX risk on dividend receivables
